conversations.title: The Changing World
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1b
conversations.instruction: Listen to a telephone conversation. Then answer the questions.

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: Hello, George.

lines.speaker: george
lines.text: Hi, Li Rui. Guess what? My father, Bob, and I are going to visit your hometown. Could you be our guide?

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: Sure! I'd love to!

lines.speaker: george
lines.text: Wonderful! You know, my father's last visit was about 20 years ago. He became a good friend of your grandfather.

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: Grandpa will be so happy. They haven't seen each other for ages!

lines.speaker: george
lines.text: Yes, and my father really wants to see how the place has changed since he last visited.

questions.prompt: Why does George call Li Rui?
What is the relationship between George's father and Li Rui's grandfather?
When did George's father last visit the place?

conversations.title: The Changing World
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1c
conversations.instruction: Li Rui is now showing George and his father around her hometown. Listen to their conversation and complete the table.

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: What do you think of your trip so far?

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: Amazing! When I first visited here, it took half a day to reach the town. Now, with the new roads, it takes only an hour.

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: Yes. When I was a child, the bus took nearly 40 minutes over rough roads to get to school. Now, it takes only 15 minutes.

lines.speaker: george
lines.text: Life is much easier!

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: Not only that. We used to have to travel far to see a doctor, but now our town has built a new hospital.

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: Great! And didn't I see a supermarket down the street?

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: Yes. We didn't have any good places for shopping until 10 years ago.

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: But the biggest change is on the hills. They used to be covered with sand.

lines.speaker: li_rui
lines.text: And now they have been covered with beautiful trees!

present.before: With the new roads, it takes only

past.before: Going to school by bus took

present.before: The town has built a new

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: I'm amazed by how green your hometown has become!

lines.speaker: grandpa
lines.text: Have you seen the corn fields outside of the town yet?

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: Yeah! They're huge!

lines.speaker: grandpa
lines.text: There used to be sandstorms all the time, and the fields were getting smaller and smaller. Then, after one big sandstorm, we decided to do something.

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: What did you do?

lines.speaker: grandpa
lines.text: We found a small bush. It had long roots that could hold the soil together. We began planting it in sandy areas. The government provided support too.

lines.speaker: bob
lines.text: Wasn't it difficult to plant so many bushes?

lines.speaker: grandpa
lines.text: Yes, it was. At the beginning, we worked by hand. Later, we had machines to help. We have planted many bushes and trees since then. There are fewer sandstorms now and the environment is getting better. It has also improved our lives. Everyone has become richer and happier.
